evidence.rule_summary_from_author: DataRemovalMechanism has one Admin SP row. ActiveDataRemovalMechanism uses the data_removal_mechanism enum, where Cryptographic Erase value 2 is mandatory, reserved enum values such as 4 are not supported and fail Set with INVALID_PARAMETER, and the Set ACL for ActiveDataRemovalMechanism is Admins OR SID.

source_snippets.text: 3.1.1.6.3 Supported Data Removal Mechanism Definition Each bit of the Supported Data Removal Mechanism (see Table 10) SHALL be set to one if the TPer supports the corresponding Data Removal Mechanism; otherwise, each bit SHALL be set to zero. The TPer SHALL support the Crypto Erase mechanism and MAY support the Overwrite Data Erase or Block Erase or other mechanisms. The TPer MAY support multiple Data Removal Mechanisms described in Table 10. After a RevertSP method has completed without an error, the condition of user data SHALL be indicated as specified in Table 10. Table 10 - Supported Data Removal Mechanism ```json [ { "Bit": 0, "Name": "Overwrite Data Erase", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "The Overwrite Data Erase mechanism causes TPer to alter information by writing a vendor specific data pattern to the medium." }, { "Bit": 1, "Name": "Block Erase", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "The Block Erase mechanism causes the TPer to alter information by setting the physical blocks to a vendor specific value." }, { "Bit": 2, "Name": "Cryptographic Erase", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "The TPer SHALL support this data erasure mechanism. Further this mechanism SHALL be executed in addition to any other supported data removal mechanism that is being executed. This bit MAY be used by the Revert or the RevertSP or the GenKey (band erase) mechanisms of data removal, where the cryptographic keys used to encrypt the user data are changed." }, { "Bit": "3-4", "Name": "Reserved", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "" }, { "Bit": 5, "Name": "Vendor Specific Erase", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "The Vendor Specific Erase mechanisms cause all user data to be removed by a vendor specific method." }, { "Bit": "6-7", "Name": "Reserved", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "" } ] ```

4.2.5.1 Data_removal_mechanism The data_removal_mechanism type is defined in Table 30 for Opal: Table 30 - data_removal_mechanism Type Table Addition ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 05 00 00 04 20", "Name": "data_removal_mechanism", "Format": "Enumeration_Type, 0, 7" } ] ``` Table 31 defines the enumeration values. The mechanisms associated with each Enumeration Value are defined in Table 10. Table 31 - data_removal_mechanism Enumeration Values ```json [ { "Enumeration Value": 0, "Associated Value": "Overwrite Data Erase" }, { "Enumeration Value": 1, "Associated Value": "Block Erase" }, { "Enumeration Value": 2, "Associated Value": "Cryptographic Erase" }, { "Enumeration Value": "3–4", "Associated Value": "Reserved" }, { "Enumeration Value": 5, "Associated Value": "Vendor Specific Erase" }, { "Enumeration Value": "6-7", "Associated Value": "Reserved" } ] ```

4.2.6.1 DataRemovalMechanism (ObjectTable) The DataRemovalMchanism table is defined in Table 32 Table 32 - DataRemovalMechansim Table Description ```json [ { "Column Number": "0x00", "Column Name": "UID", "IsUnique": "", "Column Type": "uid" }, { "Column Number": "0x01", "Column Name": "ActiveDataRemovalMechanism", "IsUnique": "", "Column Type": "data_removal_mechanism" } ] ```

4.2.6.1.2 ActiveDataRemovalMechanism This column value selects which Data Removal Mechanism in the Supported Data Removal Mechanism field in the Supported Data Removal Mechanism feature descriptor is active and will be used to remove data upon execution of the Revert method, the RevertSP method or the GenKey method. If an attempt is made to set the ActiveDataRemovalMechanism column value to an unsupported value of the data_removal_mechanism type, then the Set method invocation SHALL result in the method failing with the status INVALID_PARAMETER.

4.2.7.1 DataRemovalMechansim (M) The DataRemovalMechanism table SHALL contain exactly one row with UID = 0x00 00 11 01 00 00 00 01. The DataRemovalMechanism table SHALL be supported (see Table 33). Table 33 - Admin SP – DataRemovalMechansim Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 11 01 00 00 00 01", "ActiveDataRemovalMechanism": "VU" } ] ```

... 7", "MethodID": "Get", "CommonName": "", "ACL": "ACE_Anybody", "Log": "", "AddACEACL": "", "RemoveACEACL": "", "GetACLACL": "ACE_Anybody", "DeleteMethodACL": "", "ACLAddACELog": "", "RemoveACELog": "", "GetACLLog": "", "DeleteMethodLog": "", "LogTo": "" }, { "UID": "", "InvokingID": "00 00 02 05 TT TT TT TT *AC7", "MethodID": "Revert", "CommonName": "", "ACL": "ACE_SP_SID, ACE_Admin", "Log": "", "AddACEACL": "", "RemoveACEACL": "", "GetACLACL": "ACE_Anybody", "DeleteMethodACL": "", "ACLAddACELog": "", "RemoveACELog": "", "GetACLLog": "", "DeleteMethodLog": "", "LogTo": "" }, { "UID": "", "InvokingID": "00 00 02 05 TT TT TT TT *AC7", "MethodID": "Activate", "CommonName": "", "ACL": "ACE_SP_SID", "Log": "", "AddACEACL": "", "RemoveACEACL": "", "GetACLACL": "ACE_Anybody", "DeleteMethodACL": "", "ACLAddACELog": "", "RemoveACELog": "", "GetACLLog": "", "DeleteMethodLog": "", "LogTo": "" }, { "UID": "", "InvokingID": "00 00 11 01 00 00 00 01", "MethodID": "Get", "CommonName": "", "ACL": "ACE_Anybody", "Log": "", "AddACEACL": "", "RemoveACEACL": "", "GetACLACL": "ACE_Anybody", "DeleteMethodACL": "", "ACLAddACELog": "", "RemoveACELog": "", "GetACLLog": "", "DeleteMethodLog": "", "LogTo": "" }, { "UID": "", "InvokingID": "00 00 11 01 00 00 00 01", "MethodID": "Set", "CommonName": "", "ACL": "ACE_DataRemovalMechanism_Set_ActiveDataRemovalMechanism", "Log": "", "AddACEACL": "", "RemoveACEACL": "", "GetACLACL": "ACE_Anybody", "DeleteMethodACL": "", "ACLAddACELog": "", "RemoveACELog": "", "GetACLLog": "", "DeleteMethodLog": "", "LogTo": "" } ] ```

... on ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 00 00 01", "Name": "ACE_Anybody", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "Anybody", "Columns": "All" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 00 00 02", "Name": "ACE_Admin", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "Admins", "Columns": "All" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 03 00 01", "Name": "ACE_Set_Enabled", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "SID", "Columns": "Enabled" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 00 8C 02", "Name": "ACE_C_PIN_SID_Get_NOPIN", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "Admins OR SID", "Columns": "UID, CharSet, TryLimit, Tries, Persistence" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 00 8C 03", "Name": "ACE_C_PIN_SID_Set_PIN", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "SID", "Columns": "PIN" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 00 8C 04", "Name": "ACE_C_PIN_MSID_Get_PIN", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "Anybody", "Columns": "UID, PIN" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 03 A0 01", "Name": "ACE_C_PIN_Admins_Set_PIN", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "Admins OR SID", "Columns": "PIN" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 03 00 03", "Name": "ACE_TPerInfo_Set_ProgrammaticResetEnable", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "SID", "Columns": "ProgrammaticResetEnable" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 03 00 02", "Name": "ACE_SP_SID", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "SID", "Columns": "All" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 08 00 05 00 01", "Name": "ACE_DataRemovalMechanism_Set_ActiveDataRemovalMechanism", "CommonName": "", "BooleanExpr": "Admins OR SID", "Columns": "ActiveDataRemovalMechanism" } ] ```

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: DataRemovalMechanism has one Admin SP row. ActiveDataRemovalMechanism uses the data_removal_mechanism enum, where Cryptographic Erase value 2 is mandatory, reserved enum values such as 4 are not supported and fail Set with INVALID_PARAMETER, and the Set ACL for ActiveDataRemovalMechanism is Admins OR SID.

source_snippets.text: 3.1.1.6.3 Supported Data Removal Mechanism Definition Each bit of the Supported Data Removal Mechanism (see Table 10) SHALL be set to one if the TPer supports the corresponding Data Removal Mechanism; otherwise, each bit SHALL be set to zero. The TPer SHALL support the Crypto Erase mechanism and MAY support the Overwrite Data Erase or Block Erase or other mechanisms. The TPer MAY support multiple Data Removal Mechanisms described in Table 10. After a RevertSP method has completed without an error, the condition of user data SHALL be indicated as specified in Table 10. Table 10 - Supported Data Removal Mechanism ```json [ { "Bit": 0, "Name": "Overwrite Data Erase", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "The Overwrite Data Erase mechanism causes TPer to alter information by writing a vendor specific data pattern to the medium." }, { "Bit": 1, "Name": "Block Erase", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "The Block Erase mechanism causes the TPer to alter information by setting the physical blocks to a vendor specific value." }, { "Bit": 2, "Name": "Cryptographic Erase", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "The TPer SHALL support this data erasure mechanism. Further this mechanism SHALL be executed in addition to any other supported data removal mechanism that is being executed. This bit MAY be used by the Revert or the RevertSP or the GenKey (band erase) mechanisms of data removal, where the cryptographic keys used to encrypt the user data are changed." }, { "Bit": "3-4", "Name": "Reserved", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "" }, { "Bit": 5, "Name": "Vendor Specific Erase", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "The Vendor Specific Erase mechanisms cause all user data to be removed by a vendor specific method." }, { "Bit": "6-7", "Name": "Reserved", "Condition of user data after Data Removal": "" } ] ```

4.2.5.1 Data_removal_mechanism The data_removal_mechanism type is defined in Table 30 for Opal: Table 30 - data_removal_mechanism Type Table Addition ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 05 00 00 04 20", "Name": "data_removal_mechanism", "Format": "Enumeration_Type, 0, 7" } ] ``` Table 31 defines the enumeration values. The mechanisms associated with each Enumeration Value are defined in Table 10. Table 31 - data_removal_mechanism Enumeration Values ```json [ { "Enumeration Value": 0, "Associated Value": "Overwrite Data Erase" }, { "Enumeration Value": 1, "Associated Value": "Block Erase" }, { "Enumeration Value": 2, "Associated Value": "Cryptographic Erase" }, { "Enumeration Value": "3–4", "Associated Value": "Reserved" }, { "Enumeration Value": 5, "Associated Value": "Vendor Specific Erase" }, { "Enumeration Value": "6-7", "Associated Value": "Reserved" } ] ```

4.2.6.1 DataRemovalMechanism (ObjectTable) The DataRemovalMchanism table is defined in Table 32 Table 32 - DataRemovalMechansim Table Description ```json [ { "Column Number": "0x00", "Column Name": "UID", "IsUnique": "", "Column Type": "uid" }, { "Column Number": "0x01", "Column Name": "ActiveDataRemovalMechanism", "IsUnique": "", "Column Type": "data_removal_mechanism" } ] ```

4.2.6.1.2 ActiveDataRemovalMechanism This column value selects which Data Removal Mechanism in the Supported Data Removal Mechanism field in the Supported Data Removal Mechanism feature descriptor is active and will be used to remove data upon execution of the Revert method, the RevertSP method or the GenKey method. If an attempt is made to set the ActiveDataRemovalMechanism column value to an unsupported value of the data_removal_mechanism type, then the Set method invocation SHALL result in the method failing with the status INVALID_PARAMETER.

4.2.7.1 DataRemovalMechansim (M) The DataRemovalMechanism table SHALL contain exactly one row with UID = 0x00 00 11 01 00 00 00 01. The DataRemovalMechanism table SHALL be supported (see Table 33). Table 33 - Admin SP – DataRemovalMechansim Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 11 01 00 00 00 01", "ActiveDataRemovalMechanism": "VU" } ] ```
